pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'Introduction to Biology' (BIO101); confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which query for course with shortname 'BIO101'
Task description explicitly states Name: 'Alpha Team Coordination'; confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which reference this exact chat name
Task description explicitly states access restriction for group 'Project Team Alpha'; confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which reference this exact group name

Task description states 'repeating at the same time every week'. According to Moodle documentation and verifier.py comments, mdl_chat.schedule field values are: 0=Don't publish, 1=No repeats, 2=Daily, 3=Weekly. Value 3 corresponds to weekly scheduling as required by the task.
Task description explicitly states 'Save past sessions: 180 days'; confirmed in export_result.sh which queries the mdl_chat.keepdays field
Task description explicitly states 'Next session on the upcoming Friday at 14:00 (2:00 PM)'; 14:00 in 24-hour format equals hour value 14

Task description states 'Next session on the upcoming Friday'; Python's datetime.weekday() returns 0=Monday through 6=Sunday, so Friday=4. This is confirmed by verifier.py comment on line 32: 'Friday is 4'

privileged_info_summary: This task requires creating a chat activity in a Moodle LMS instance. All metadata values are consistent with the task requirements: the course shortname is BIO101 ('Introduction to Biology'), the chat activity must be named 'Alpha Team Coordination', access must be restricted to the group 'Project Team Alpha', the schedule type must be weekly (value 3), chat sessions must be kept for 180 days, and the chat time must be set to Friday (weekday 4) at 14:00 (hour 14). The task uses the Moodle database fields mdl_chat.schedule (where 3=Weekly), mdl_chat.keepdays (set to 180), and mdl_course_modules.availability for group-based access restrictions.
